name: Coastal gravel route from Zarautz to Lekeitio
name_original: Ruta gravel costera de Zarautz a Lekeitio
description: This difficult 33.8-mile (54.5 km) gravel route from Zarautz to Lekeitio offers challenging climbs and stunning Basque Coast views.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Zarautz offers diverse landscapes, from challenging coastal routes to scenic paths through vineyards and rural areas. The terrain often includes a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with significant elevation gains in many areas. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Cantabrian Sea, rolling hills dotted with txakoli vineyards, and the foothills of the Basque mountains. These features provide a varied and demanding environment for gravel cyclists. Best gravel bike trails around Zarautz The most popular gravel bike trail is Coastal gravel route from Zarautz to Lekeitio, a 33.8 miles (54.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete. This route features challenging climbs and views of the Basque Coast. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is View over Zarautz and the coast – View of Zarautz Bay loop from Aia-Orio, a difficult 19.8 miles (31.8 km) path. This route offers panoramic views of Zarautz Bay and the Cantabrian Sea. Local gravel bikers also love the Oria River – Andoain loop from Asteasu, a 28.1 miles (45.3 km) trail leading through the Oria River valley, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Gravel biking around Zarautz is defined by coastal paths, txakoli vineyards, and mountain trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature significant elevation. The routes in Zarautz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 70 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Zarautz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Zarautz?
A: There are several challenging gravel bike trails around Zarautz. Our guide currently features 4 distinct routes, all rated as difficult, offering diverse experiences from coastal rides to inland loops.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Zarautz?
A: Gravel biking in Zarautz offers a varied terrain, typically a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. You'll encounter rolling hills, coastal paths, and routes through txakoli vineyards and rural areas. Many routes feature significant elevation gains, requiring good fitness.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly gravel routes in Zarautz?
A: The gravel routes currently featured in our guide for Zarautz are all rated as difficult, involving significant elevation gains and challenging terrain. While there are no easy routes listed, the region's diverse landscape might offer shorter, less strenuous sections suitable for beginners if explored locally.

Q: What are some of the most scenic viewpoints or natural features I can see while gravel biking?
A: Zarautz's gravel trails offer breathtaking scenery. You can enjoy panoramic views of Zarautz Bay and the Cantabrian Sea on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View over Zarautz and the coast – View of Zarautz Bay loop from Aia-Orio&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes pass through picturesque txakoli vineyards and offer glimpses of the Iñurritza Protected Biotope and the nearby Zumaia Flysch Cliffs, a globally recognized geological marvel.

Q: Can I find circular gravel bike routes in the Zarautz area?
A: Yes, several gravel routes around Zarautz are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View over Zarautz and the coast – View of Zarautz Bay loop from Aia-Orio&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oria River – Andoain loop from Asteasu&lt;/a&gt; are excellent circular options, allowing you to start and finish at the same point.

Q: What is the best time of year to go gravel biking in Zarautz?
A: Zarautz is an excellent destination for outdoor activities. While specific seasonal recommendations for gravel biking aren't provided, the Basque Country generally enjoys pleasant weather from spring through autumn. Summers are warm, and coastal routes can be refreshing. Always check local weather conditions before heading out, especially for routes with significant elevation.

Q: Where can I park my car when accessing gravel trails in Zarautz?
A: While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, Zarautz is a well-established town with various parking options. For routes starting directly from Zarautz, you can typically find parking within the town or at designated spots near popular access points. For routes starting from nearby villages, look for local public parking areas.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about the trails in Zarautz?
A: The gravel biking routes around Zarautz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 70 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Zarautz's varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs, stunning coastal views, and diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or landmarks I should look out for along the routes?
A: Absolutely! While gravel biking, you might pass by or have views of several notable attractions. Keep an eye out for the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1101604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zumaia Flysch Cliffs&lt;/a&gt;, the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/709446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orio Old Town and Harbor&lt;/a&gt;, or the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4503659" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarautz Beach&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features various peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/680276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount San Antón&lt;/a&gt;, offering great vantage points.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced gravel bikers?
A: Yes, Zarautz is ideal for experienced gravel bikers seeking a challenge. All routes in our guide are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and demanding terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1671585497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal gravel route from Zarautz to Lekeitio&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a 33.8-mile trail with over 1000 meters of ascent, offering challenging climbs and stunning Basque Coast views.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through the famous txakoli vineyards?
A: Yes, the hillsides surrounding Zarautz are dotted with txakoli vineyards, and several routes lead through these picturesque areas. While not explicitly named in every route description, the inland loops and mountain climbs often traverse these beautiful green landscapes, offering a unique cultural and scenic experience.

Q: Is public transport an option for reaching trailheads in Zarautz?
A: Zarautz is a well-connected coastal town in the Basque Country. While specific public transport links to every trailhead are not provided, the town itself is accessible by train and bus, making it possible to reach central starting points for many routes. For routes further afield, local bus services to surrounding villages might be an option.
tips: The mouse of Getaria is how Mount San Antón is popularly known. Currently you can climb to its top taking a nice walk. From it on a sunny day you will see magnificent views of the town and the Guipuzcoan coast.
The city is beautifully situated between the cliffs and there are cycling and walking paths everywhere

Downhill you find a very nice and interesting geological park with hiking trail along the coast and extraordinary views to the sea! The speciality is the Flysch which rises up high and straight from the sea.
https://www.komoot.de/tour/89063118

From this specially conditioned viewpoint on the road, you can enjoy the panoramic view of Deva and its Santiago beach. On the other side of the estuary, its counterpart in Mutriku, Ondarbeltz beach. One of the main characteristics of both beaches is the exposure of the rock strata on the surface devoid of sand, a typical landscape throughout the Basque coast.

Lekeitio beach is called Isuntza and is located to the east of the Biscayan coast. Being located in the same urban area, next to the port and the local park, Isuntza has numerous walking areas, bars, restaurants and hotels in its surroundings.

In addition, Lekeitio beach has lifeguards and urban parking. It also has public toilets, sinks, showers, changing rooms, bike racks and footbaths.

Lekeitio is an emblem for Bizkaia and the Basque Country in general. A town with a fishing tradition with a beautiful old town. Its fishermen's houses with colorful balconies and port will captivate you.
Good views and the cafe is nice for a coffee/beer/snack with a view

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this gravel route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a hard gravel ride. It requires a very good fitness level due to significant elevation gain and some potentially technical sections. Be prepared for some steep uphill segments where you might need to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, offering a diverse gravel biking adventure. You'll encounter mostly paved surfaces, but also compacted gravel, some cobblestones, and even singletrack sections. The route transitions between smooth coastal roads, green hills, and forested areas.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the rugged beauty of the Basque Coast with extensive Atlantic panoramas. Notable highlights include the prominent natural monument Ratón de Getaria, the picturesque town of Zumaia, and the unique geological formations of the Sakoneta Flysch Cliffs. You'll also pass through charming coastal villages like Getaria and Mutriku, ending in Lekeitio, and see the Santa Catalina Lighthouse.

faqs.question: Is this a loop route or point-to-point?
faqs.answer: This is a point-to-point route, starting in Zarautz and concluding in Lekeitio. You will need to arrange transportation back to your starting point or onward from Lekeitio.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The Basque Country coast offers pleasant conditions for cycling during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the crowds are thinner. Summer can be warm, but the coastal breeze often helps. Winter riding is possible but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially wet weather.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the route in Zarautz?
faqs.answer: Zarautz has various parking options, including public parking lots near the beach or town center. It's advisable to check local parking regulations and availability, especially during peak season, as some areas may require payment or have time limits.

faqs.question: Can I reach the starting point by public transport?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zarautz is well-connected by public transport. You can typically reach Zarautz by Euskotren (local train service) from San Sebastián, which is a convenient option for cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this gravel route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor areas in the Basque Country are dog-friendly, specific regulations can vary along this route, especially when passing through towns, beaches, or protected natural areas like the Flysch Cliffs. It's best to keep dogs on a leash and be mindful of local signage. Always carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or fees for cycling through the Basque Coast Geopark?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific entrance fees for cycling through the Basque Coast Geopark. However, as a protected natural area, it's crucial to stick to marked trails, respect the environment, and avoid disturbing wildlife. Some specific areas or visitor centers within the Geopark might have their own regulations or small fees for certain activities, but cycling on the main route is typically unrestricted.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Northern Route (Basque Country), Donostia - Mutriku, and Geoparkeko Bira - Deba-Zumaia, among others. This allows for potential extensions or variations if you wish to explore further.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The route passes through or near several charming coastal towns like Getaria, Zumaia, and Mutriku, and ends in Lekeitio. These towns offer numerous opportunities to stop for a coffee, grab a meal, or even try local specialties like Txakoli wine. It's a great way to experience the local culture and refuel.
